cards.title: new-to-do-app effect
cards.content:
- *"this new app will fix everything"*
- however, you can utilize this — this initial burst of energy is not without value

cards.title: project churn rate
cards.content: a concept from Cal Newport, essentially asking "how many projects are you getting done per time unit?"

cards.title: restart complexity
cards.content:
**how much effort, thought and labor does it cost to get the system back up and running once abandoned?**

- common reason of system failure (and not a high threshold required)
- known it from [📰 Apply Weakest Link Theory to To Productivity Systems](https://www.calnewport.com/blog/2007/07/23/monday-master-class-apply-weakest-link-theory-to-time-management)

cards.title: just do it
cards.content:
> So stop thinking about it. Stop dreaming about it. Stop researching every aspect of it and reading all about it and debating the pros and cons of it … Start doing it.

— 📖 Discipline Equals Freedom

cards.title: > You can step back onto the path of mastery anytime you want.
cards.content: — The Slight Edge

cards.title: jump point
cards.content:
- **when doing something (/starting something), there comes a critical point where you have to "jump", and any strategy or tactic that is not super basic will not help you**
- *as in:* drawing a complex flow chart with your gym plan for the next 5 months will not help you in that very moment where you have have to stop Netflix and get up

![sketch of man hesitating to jump from a cliff](../media/img/personal-productivity-philosophy/jump.webp)

- concept by James Lim

cards.title: ~ we are wildly optimistic in predicting how productive we will be in the near future
cards.content: — 2011 📖 The Willpower Instinct

cards.title: gumption trap
cards.content: situation that kills your motivation (/flow) while working towards a goal

cards.title: > To initiate action, doing must be easier than thinking
cards.content: — 📖 Hooked: How to Build Habit-Forming Products

cards.title: ~ attraction towards the mindless is as old as time
cards.content:
> Learning to live, then as now, is hard work.

> the depth I preach, both in work and personal affairs, is not a default mode subverted only recently by new technology

— [📰 Arnold Bennett’s Fight Against Steampunk Social Media](https://www.calnewport.com/blog/2017/11/03/arnold-bennetts-fight-against-steampunk-social-media/)

cards.title: ~ to guard your focus, sweep for mines
cards.content:
> Sweep for mines. Turn off your phone, shut down your e-mail, and exit your Internet browser. Your most important work deserves 100 percent of your attention. 

— 📖 The One Thing

cards.title: Mixed Feelings Park
cards.content:
when you procrastinate a lot first, then do your thing, but the time waste was huge, and so the outcome is so-so


— concept from [How to Beat Procrastination (waitbutwhy)](https://waitbutwhy.com/2013/11/how-to-beat-procrastination.html)

cards.title: unachievable goal/moonshot
cards.content: ![](../media/img/personal-productivity-philosophy/goal.webp)

cards.title: ~ committing to do an habit daily means there will be slip ups
cards.content:
— [“Write Every Day” is Bad Advice — Hacking the Psychology of Big Projects](https://www.calnewport.com/blog/2013/01/13/write-every-day-is-bad-advice-hacking-the-psychology-of-big-projects/)

...and streaks become more "emotionally dangerous" (when they fail) the longer they go on...

cards.title: input-orientation
cards.content: - **thinking about productivity in terms of input, e.g. *> The one thing we control is the time we put into a task***

cards.title: After Action Report
cards.content:
> After Action Reports (AARs), which serve as live autopsies. We do them no matter the outcome, and if you’re analyzing a failure like I was, the AAR is absolutely crucial.

— 📖 Can't Hurt Me

cards.title: one day break becomes two days becomes indefinite
remember the hockey stick
cards.content: *goes slow at the beginning, then faster and faster* (as long as it's actually exponential, which not all things are)

cards.title: ~ working 10% more produces twice the output
cards.content:
> Given two people of approximately the same ability and one person who works ten percent more than the other, the latter will more than twice outproduce the former

— [You and Your Research](https://www.cs.virginia.edu/~robins/YouAndYourResearch.html)

cards.title: productivity hacking tarpit
cards.content: - from [this HN comment](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=24916536#24927225)

cards.title: ~ do not confuse movement with progress
cards.content: - from [this HN comment](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=24916536#24927225)

cards.title: Hemingway Bridge
cards.content:
- *stop working only when you know what the next step will be*

- mentioned in *📖 Building A Second Brain*

cards.title: ~ analysis paralysis is dangerous in strategy searching
cards.content:
> One reason that we avoid choosing a strategy is that we’re not comfortable walking away from all the other possible strategies. Rather than celebrate the paths not taken, we take no path at all. 

— 📖 This Is Strategy

cards.title: > An artist gives all they have to the art
cards.content: — Blue Eye Samurai

cards.title: Everything worth doing is worth doing well vs. Everything worth doing is worth doing badly
> Time without attention is worthless, so value attention over time.
cards.content: — Tim Ferris

cards.title: > cull your inputs
cards.content: — [On Claude Shannon's Deliberate Depth](https://www.calnewport.com/blog/2017/07/21/on-claude-shannons-deliberate-depth/)

cards.title: > You have around 2 weeks before you lose motivation to work on something
cards.content: — [Making a Popular Open Source  Project](https://skerritt.blog/make-popular-open-source-projects/)

cards.title: ~ use weekly planning as a method to squelch unrealistic expectations
cards.content: — inspired by [Deep Habits: How a Big City Lawyer Uses Weekly Planning to Accomplish More in 45 Hours Than Most Could Accomplish in 100](https://www.calnewport.com/blog/2014/08/19/deep-habits-how-a-big-city-lawyer-uses-weekly-planning-to-accomplish-more-in-45-hours-than-most-could-accomplish-in-100/)

cards.title: lower the bar
cards.content:
(to fight writer's block/perfectionism)

- idea from *📖 Writing Tools - 50 Essential Strategies for Every Writer*

cards.title: ~ making people change intentions is easy, making them change behavior is not
cards.content:
and: *~ medium-to-large intention change leads to small-to-medium behavior change*

— *📝 Does changing behavioral intentions engender behavior change﹖ A meta-analysis of the experimental evidence.*

cards.title: > If you do not work on an important problem, it's unlikely you'll do important work
cards.content: — You and Your Research

cards.title: ~ reduce number of decision you have to make
cards.content: — Roy Baumeister (?)

cards.title: ~ don't make complex plans ignoring the emotions of performing
cards.content: — [📀 How to stop making EXCUSES when working on your goals](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8RKtCXheAjY)

cards.title: ~ you have to have counters for excuses well-memorized at the jump point
cards.content: — [📀 How to stop making EXCUSES when working on your goals](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8RKtCXheAjY)
